title: The General
content:
"Good-morning; good-morning!" the General said
    When we met him last week on our way to the line.
    Now the soldiers he smiled at are most of 'em dead,
    And we're cursing his staff for incompetent swine.
    "He's a cheery old card," grunted Harry to Jack
    As they slogged up to Arras with rifle and pack.
     *        *        *        *        *
    But he did for them both by his plan of attack.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Siegfried Loraine Sassoon, titled "The General", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
